title: polypogon × littoralis var. interruptus
intro: Polypogon × littoralis var. interruptus, also known as interrupted meadow-grass or sea bent, is a hybrid plant species derived from the cross between Polypogon monspeliensis and Lolium perenne. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, making it useful in treating wounds, skin irritations, and minor infections. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Traditionally, Polypogon × littoralis var. interruptus has been used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, although its modern use and availability may be limited due to its relatively unknown status in contemporary herbalism.
